message.content: Adversaries may deploy containers within an environment to facilitate execution or evade defenses. This technique involves deploying a new container to execute processes associated with a particular image or deployment, such as downloading or running malware. Containers can be configured without network rules or user limitations to bypass existing defenses. Deployment methods include Docker's create and start APIs, Kubernetes dashboard, or Kubeflow. Adversaries may use malicious images or benign images that download and execute malicious payloads at runtime. Notable examples include Doki, Kinsing, and Peirates, which have been used to execute commands or create reverse shells. TeamTNT has deployed containers for cryptocurrency mining in Kubernetes clusters. Mitigation strategies include monitoring container creation and start events, enforcing strict network and user policies, and using security tools to scan container images for vulnerabilities and malicious content.
